qualitative_claim: Very low volume at a meaningful edge marks buyers or sellers running out of ammunition.
bullish_rule: At swing low or low of day/support, edge volume at low is below exhaustion limit and signal bar closes; enter long when next bar trades up.
bearish_rule: At swing high or high of day/resistance, edge volume at high is below exhaustion limit and signal bar closes; enter short when next bar trades down.
required_data: OHLCV; footprint volume at price; session high/low; swing high/low
feature_columns_needed: edge_volume_low; edge_volume_high; is_swing_low; is_swing_high; is_lod; is_hod; next_bar_direction; signal_bar_range_ticks
candidate_thresholds: Directly supported starting point: swing lookback 25 and edge volume limit 10 for ES model; relax by market/session.
confirmation_rule: Bar close confirms signal; next bar trades at least 1 tick in reversal direction.
invalidation_rule: Signal extreme breached and accepted; no favorable movement after several bars/minutes.
expected_edge: High: tight stop beyond edge and immediate rejection expected.

qualitative_claim: The best exhaustion reversal improves when it follows absorption or prior aggressive flow failure.
bullish_rule: After passive-buyer absorption or negative-delta green candle near low, require exhaustion print at/near support and next-bar lift.
bearish_rule: After passive-seller absorption or positive-delta failure near high, require exhaustion print at/near resistance and next-bar drop.
required_data: OHLCV; footprint; delta; bid/ask volume; levels
feature_columns_needed: absorption_score; exhaustion_print; level_proximity_ticks; delta_contradiction; next_bar_direction; stop_distance_ticks
candidate_thresholds: No hard threshold; use graded absorption_score plus H001 exhaustion settings as configurable defaults.
confirmation_rule: Signal bar closes with exhaustion; price rotates away from absorbed zone within 1-3 bars.
invalidation_rule: Price accepts through absorbed zone or signal extreme; aggressive side continues.
expected_edge: Very high: combines flagship setup pieces.

qualitative_claim: Exhaustion is stronger when directional delta weakens into the new extreme.
bullish_rule: Lower low or retest low has less negative delta than prior sell bars, then exhaustion print appears at low.
bearish_rule: Higher high or retest high has less positive or negative delta versus prior buy bars, then exhaustion print appears at high.
required_data: OHLCV; bar delta; max/min delta; footprint edge volume
feature_columns_needed: delta_slope_lookback; delta_at_extreme; prior_push_delta; edge_volume; price_extreme_type; exhaustion_print
candidate_thresholds: Use normalized delta versus prior 3-5 directional bars; no fixed contract threshold.
confirmation_rule: Next bar trades away from the extreme; early MFE appears quickly.
invalidation_rule: Delta re-accelerates in trend direction and price extends through signal extreme.
expected_edge: High: catches effort fading at edge.

qualitative_claim: Negative delta can be bullish when sellers are absorbed by passive buyers and price holds.
bullish_rule: At low/support, candle closes green or off low with negative delta and poor downside progress; long on rotation above signal high or next-bar lift.
bearish_rule: Not applicable except mirror logic in H005.

candidate_thresholds: No hard threshold; use delta percentile and price-progress failure score.
confirmation_rule: Price lifts away from absorbed low and does not revisit/accept below it.
invalidation_rule: Price breaks and accepts below absorbed low; selling delta expands.
expected_edge: High: captures passive buying at lows.

qualitative_claim: Strong positive delta at resistance can be bearish if aggressive buyers are absorbed and there is no continuation.
bullish_rule: Not applicable except mirror logic in H004.
bearish_rule: At high/resistance, strong positive delta fails to produce continuation, candle rejects or next bar turns negative; short on loss of signal low.

qualitative_claim: Breakouts that cannot hold outside a meaningful level often reverse.
bullish_rule: After breakdown below prior low/support/value area, price closes back above level with order-flow failure; long on reclaim.
bearish_rule: After breakout above prior high/resistance/value area, price closes back below level with order-flow failure; short on reclaim.

invalidation_rule: Retest holds outside level or price accepts beyond breakout.
expected_edge: High when level is obvious and failure is fast.

qualitative_claim: A sweep beyond a visible high/low that immediately reclaims can mark exhaustion of stop liquidity.
bullish_rule: Sweep below prior low with volume/delta spike, then reclaim prior low; long after reclaim.
bearish_rule: Sweep above prior high with volume/delta spike, then reject below prior high; short after reclaim.

feature_columns_needed: sweep_direction; sweep_ticks; volume_spike_percentile; delta_spike_percentile; reclaim_flag; wick_ratio
candidate_thresholds: Use relative volume/delta percentile, not fixed size; default volume percentile 90 as research starting point.
confirmation_rule: Price reclaims swept level and moves away within 1-3 bars.
invalidation_rule: Level flips into support/resistance for continuation; fresh imbalances continue sweep direction.
expected_edge: Medium-high but noisy: stop volume is ambiguous.

qualitative_claim: Late aggressive traders at poor location become fuel when their flow is absorbed and fails.

bearish_rule: Strong buy delta/volume into high fails to continue; price reverses through likely long-entry zone; short.

bearish_rule: Higher high with less positive delta or cumulative delta divergence at resistance; short after reversal confirmation.

qualitative_claim: Stacked imbalances should create follow-through; failure at an edge is reversal evidence.

bearish_rule: Stacked buy imbalance into high/resistance does not continue higher and price loses stack zone; short.

invalidation_rule: Clean continuation in stack direction; retest holds in stack direction.
expected_edge: Medium: useful but context-sensitive.

invalidation_rule: Strong trend continuation features remain intact.
expected_edge: Reduces false positives materially.

confirmation_rule: Only allow if post-open confirmation appears.
invalidation_rule: Pre-open signal gets chopped/stopped by open volatility.
expected_edge: Reduces bad timing trades.
